books.title: Black Hat Python
books.author: Justin Seitz
books.description: Discover the darker side of Python programming. Learn how to write network sniffers, manipulate packets, infect virtual machines, and create stealthy trojans using Python 3.

books.title: Linux Basics for Hackers
books.author: OccupyTheWeb
books.description: An practical introduction to the Linux operating system designed specifically for security professionals and ethical hackers. Master terminal basics, bash scripting, and system administration.

books.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook
books.author: Dafydd Stuttard & Marcus Pinto
books.description: The definitive guide to finding and exploiting security flaws in web applications. Step-by-step walkthroughs for bypassing modern defense mechanisms and securing applications.

books.title: Practical Malware Analysis
books.author: Michael Sikorski & Andrew Honig
books.description: The hands-on guide to dissecting malicious software. Learn how to set up isolated safe environments, dissect code structure, analyze network traffic, and build custom detection tools.

books.title: Hacking APIs
books.author: Corey J. Ball
books.description: Explore the critical vulnerabilities hidden in modern API endpoints. Learn how to map API systems, execute injection attacks, scan for authentication bypasses, and construct secure integrations.

books.title: Metasploit: The Penetration Tester's Guide
books.author: David Kennedy, Jim O'Gorman, Devon Kearns
books.description: Make the most of the world's most popular penetration testing framework. Learn how to write exploit modules, execute post-exploitation modules, bypass antivirus, and manage engagements.

books.title: RTFM: Red Team Field Manual
books.author: Ben Clark
books.description: A compact, highly dense reference manual listing critical commands for red team members. Contains syntaxes for Linux, Windows, Cisco devices, networking, and common testing utilities.

books.title: Red Team Development and Operations
books.author: Joe Vest & James Tubberville
books.description: A comprehensive guide to building, managing, and executing realistic red team operations. Learn how to design adversary simulations, control risk, and report high-impact outcomes.

books.title: Blue Team Handbook: Incident Response Edition
books.author: Don Murdoch
books.description: An essential field guide for security analysts, incident responders, and network defenders. Packed with commands, logs, configuration scripts, and checklists for fast response.

books.title: CEH Certified Ethical Hacker Study Guide
books.author: Ric Messier
books.description: Prepare for the Certified Ethical Hacker exam with practical, real-world instruction. Covers intrusion detection, web app vulnerabilities, cryptography, cloud computing, and IoT systems.

books.title: CompTIA Security+ Study Guide
books.author: Mike Chapple & David Seidl
books.description: The ultimate reference for the CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 exam. Master network security foundations, risk management, threat mitigation, identity controls, and enterprise operations.

books.title: CISSP Official Study Guide
books.author: Mike Chapple, James Stewart, Darril Gibson
books.description: The industry standard study guide for the CISSP exam. Covers security architecture, asset security, communication, operations, risk management, and identity provisioning.

books.title: Network Security Essentials
books.author: William Stallings
books.description: A clean, mathematically rigorous introduction to network security protocols, system configurations, firewalls, threat modeling, security architecture, and cryptography standard implementations.

books.title: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach
books.author: James Kurose & Keith Ross
books.description: The gold standard text on computer networking. Learn how layers interface, protocols negotiate, routers forward packets, and local area networks handle concurrency.

books.title: OWASP Web Security Testing Guide
books.author: OWASP Foundation
books.description: The open-source master guide for testing the security of web applications. Covers vulnerability mapping, input validation, logic testing, session management, and configuration audits.

books.title: Practical Packet Analysis
books.author: Chris Sanders
books.description: Learn to use Wireshark to solve real-world network problems. Master traffic capture, packet filtering, network diagnostics, protocol dissection, and performance bottleneck debugging.

books.title: Applied Cryptography
books.author: Bruce Schneier
books.description: The classic cryptographic treatise detailing protocols, algorithms, and source code. Learn to implement public-key cryptography, symmetric ciphers, and key exchange schemes safely.

books.title: Threat Modeling: Designing for Security
books.author: Adam Shostack
books.description: Learn how to predict, prevent, and fix security flaws during software design. Includes templates, frameworks (STRIDE, DREAD), and strategies for integrating threat modeling into CI/CD.

books.title: Designing Secure Software
books.author: Loren Kohnfelder
books.description: A guide for developers to write vulnerability-free code from inception. Focuses on safe API design, input filtering, authorization modeling, and secure software development lifecycles.

books.title: Alice and Bob Learn Application Security
books.author: Tanya Janca
books.description: An accessible, engaging walkthrough of application security fundamentals. Covers threat modeling, secure coding practices, static and dynamic testing tools, and DevSecOps processes.

books.title: Hacking: The Art of Exploitation
books.author: Jon Erickson
books.description: A comprehensive deep dive into shellcode, buffer overflows, format string vulnerabilities, and low-level C programming. Learn the scientific approach behind software exploitation.

books.title: Serious Cryptography
books.author: Jean-Philippe Aumasson
books.description: A practical introduction to modern encryption, hashing, and security algorithms. Learn about block ciphers, public-key systems, authenticated encryption, and potential cryptographic vulnerabilities.

books.title: Social Engineering: The Science of Human Hacking
books.author: Christopher Hadnagy
books.description: Explore the psychological methods hackers use to manipulate human targets. Learn how to identify influence tactics, defend against social engineering, and run security training audits.

books.title: Pentesting Azure Applications
books.author: Matt Burrough
books.description: A hands-on guide for security testers and cloud administrators. Discover common configuration errors in Azure Active Directory, storage accounts, microservices, and databases.

books.title: Incident Response & Computer Forensics
books.author: Jason T. Luttgens, Matthew Pepe, Kevin Mandia
books.description: The definitive resource for incident response teams. Learn how to preserve digital evidence, analyze system RAM, reconstruct timelines, and eradicate threat actors from networks.

books.title: The Shellcoder's Handbook
books.author: Chris Anley, John Heasman, Felix Linder
books.description: Learn to write kernel exploits, heap overflows, and target-specific payloads. Explores OS vulnerabilities across Windows, Linux, Solaris, and OS X environments.

books.title: Practical Social Engineering
books.author: Joe Gray
books.description: Combine open-source intelligence (OSINT) and psychology to conduct effective simulated social engineering attacks. Perfect for red teamers and cybersecurity consultants.

books.title: Operator Handbook: Red Team + Blue Team Reference
books.author: Joshua L. Pruitt
books.description: An exhaustive command sheet designed for both attackers and defenders. Lists commands, configuration tools, script templates, and quick guides for common penetration tools.

books.title: Tribe of Hackers: Cybersecurity Advice
books.author: Marcus J. Carey & Jennifer Jin
books.description: Interviews with leading cybersecurity experts sharing career advice, security philosophies, predictions, and tips for starting out in the hacking field.

books.title: Practical Digital Forensics for Linux Systems
books.author: Philip Polstra
books.description: A hands-on manual detailing forensics analysis on Linux servers and endpoints. Learn how to reconstruct logs, verify file hashes, search for hidden rootkits, and extract disk images.
